steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The motorman is frustrated (chafed) about the laborer's difficult lot. He then "registered discontent." The word "registered" must describe how his internal frustration becomes externally visible — the target meaning is to show or to display.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Bypassed’: Bypassed means went around or avoided something; the sentence says the motorman showed discontent, not avoided it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Enrolled’: Enrolled means signed up or registered as a member; the sentence is about showing an emotion, not joining a group or course. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Valued’: Valued means considered important or useful; the sentence is about making discontent visible, not appraising it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Displayed’: Displayed means made visible or evident — exactly how frustration shows on someone's face or behavior. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
